title: Journey carries CTA doors: buttons that only open existing surfaces — the page still computes and mutates nothing
context: The owner used the read-only Journey (v0.10.0), liked it, and asked for calls to action — the screen told the story but ended in a dead end. The prior decision forbade buttons outright to keep Journey honest.
decision: Journey keeps its honesty contract — the page computes nothing, mutates nothing, and every number stays copied from the graph or hunch stats --json — but may carry CTA DOORS: command: links restricted by an enableCommandUris allowlist to existing commands only (hunch.capture, hunch.why, hunch.search, hunch.reviewInTerminal). The one-next-action card becomes its own button (Review N drafts → opens `hunch review` in a terminal; else Capture a decision). Draft triage still happens in the CLI — the button just opens the terminal there. Any CTA that computes, edits records in-page, or targets a command outside the allowlist requires superseding this decision. v0.11.0.
consequences: Journey ends in an action instead of a dead end; the loop closes from the story screen.
The enableCommandUris allowlist is the enforcement seam: new doors require touching it explicitly.
hunch.reviewInTerminal exists but is hidden from the palette — it is a door, not a surface.
alternatives_rejected: In-page draft accept/reject buttons — rejected: that rebuilds the deleted review GUI; triage stays in the CLI where the full protocol lives.
postMessage + enableScripts webview — rejected: command: links need no JS; scripts would invite the page to start computing.
Keep Journey buttonless — rejected by the owner after real use: a story with no next step reads as a dead end.
